response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong software craftsmanship habits (TDD, CI/CD, testing) and relevant low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic using DLang for high-throughput services. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust as the primary technology, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (~6 months, 2014-2015) with no Rust experience. No geospatial routing or navigation-tile pipeline background, though GISCollective demonstrates genuine geospatial domain interest. Worth a screening conversation if the team values systems-thinking and willingness to ramp on C++/Rust, otherwise a pass.
strengths: Geospatial domain affinity via GISCollective (long-running open-source GIS platform)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of req/s at Sociomantic using async I/O, fibers)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, trunk-based dev, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship
concerns: Only brief C/C++ experience (~6 months, a decade ago) and no Rust — core requirement gap
No navigation/routing-tile or production map-data pipeline experience
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node and AI tooling, drifting from systems-programming track
